stem: A release enters the software release pipeline. The probability of passing code review is 97%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing unit testing is between 60% and 77% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing integration testing is 70%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing deployment is 87%. What is the overall probability that the release is deployed without incidents?

ground_truth.answer: P(deployed without incidents) ranges from 0.354 to 0.455 as the unit testing pass rate varies from 60% to 77%. Point estimate: 0.408.

solution_steps: The release must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the unit testing stage creates a 10.1 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the unit testing rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
